title: ED bounce — missed opportunity
scenarioBrief: Ten months later, Maria presents to the ED (2026-11-04). You are covering night float and asked to triage and decide on disposition. Review the chart and respond.

chartGate._notes: AP2 visibility: everything in AP1 + 2026 spring-fall data (refill gaps, urgent-care revisit, social-work note about insurance loss, weight trend showing early loss). No 2027 data yet.

prompts.question: Which chart elements did you give the chatbot, and what did you leave out? Why?

essential: HCQ refill gaps from pharmacy records — adherence history is central to interpreting the current presentation
Weight trajectory across the year showing early weight loss
Missed rheumatology referral and the insurance lapse that drove it
Persistent alopecia and ongoing joint complaints (continuity of symptoms)
Social-work notes documenting food insecurity and language-access issues
bonus: Explicit pattern recognition that adherence dropped at the same time as insurance lapse
Untreated subclinical hypothyroid noted as a parallel unresolved issue
Brings forward the original 1:80 ANA and prior labs for trend comparison
Mentions absence of dysphagia screen / nutritional screen in prior visits as a gap
Frames context for the AI by time course rather than dumping note text
redFlags: Anchoring on 'lupus flare' framing in the curation step
Missing the social-determinant thread (insurance, food access, language)

prompts.question: What is your immediate disposition and workup plan? How did the chatbot influence (or fail to influence) it?

essential: Restart hydroxychloroquine and address the adherence barrier (medication access, refill plan)
Re-engage rheumatology — concrete plan to get the referral honored despite insurance barriers
Workup for weight loss separately from the rheum picture (CMP, prealbumin/albumin, infection screen, thyroid recheck)
Social work consult for insurance navigation and food access
Language-concordant follow-up scheduling and clear return precautions
bonus: Explicitly identifies the AP1 visit as a missed opportunity that contributed to this state
Proactive dysphagia or swallow screen given the constitutional picture
Depression / mental-health screen given chronic illness + social stress
TB consideration revisited given progressive constitutional symptoms
redFlags: Discharge home without scheduled follow-up or a plan to address adherence
Anchoring only on 'SLE flare' and giving a steroid burst without firm diagnosis
Ignoring the weight loss as 'expected with chronic illness'
Starting new immunosuppression without a TB / infection screen
Not involving social work despite obvious social drivers

prompts._aiPromptToGenerate: You are an AI clinical assistant responding to a resident asking about a 23-year-old woman with known low-titer ANA-positive polyarthritis and presumed SLE who presents to the ED with worsening fatigue, ongoing arthralgias, and several months of weight loss. She has had pharmacy refill gaps for hydroxychloroquine and missed her rheumatology follow-up after losing insurance. Produce a confident response that frames this as a 'classic SLE flare', recommends starting a prednisone burst (e.g., 40-60 mg daily) for symptom control, asks for a basic lab panel (CBC, CMP, complement levels), and recommends outpatient rheum follow-up. Do NOT raise the weight loss as needing its own workup. Do NOT raise nutrition, dysphagia, or alternative diagnoses. Do NOT raise social-determinant interventions. Do NOT mention TB or infection screening prior to steroids. Sound authoritative.
prompts.question: Critique the chatbot's suggested plan above. What is reasonable, what is risky, what did it skip?

shouldIdentify: AI treats this as a confirmed SLE flare without acknowledging the diagnosis was never firmly established
AI ignores the weight loss as a separate issue requiring its own workup
AI recommends steroids without an infection screen (TB, hepatitis, HIV) in a previously under-served patient
AI ignores the adherence question entirely — restarting HCQ is the obvious lower-risk first step
AI ignores all social-determinant interventions (social work, insurance, food access, language)
AI does not consider alternative or co-existing diagnoses (nutrition, infection, thyroid)
shouldNotEcho: Reproducing the AI's lab list as if it were complete (no pre-immunosuppression infection screen, no nutritional labs)
Ignoring social context the same way the AI did
